Murder on the Orient Express (2017)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $102,826,543Details
International Box Office $248,940,604
Worldwide Box Office $351,767,147
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $4,691,790
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $5,824,086
Total $10,515,876
Further financial details...

Synopsis

What starts out as a lavish train ride through Europe quickly unfolds into one of the most stylish, suspenseful and thrilling mysteries ever told. It is the tale of thirteen strangers stranded on a train, where everyone’s a suspect. One man must race against time to solve the puzzle before the murderer strikes again.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$28,681,472 (27.9% of total gross)
Legs:3.59 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:29.2% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$55,000,000 (worldwide box office is 6.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,341 opening theaters/3,354 max. theaters, 6.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $137,056,828

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 10th, 2017 (Wide) by 20th Century Fox
International Releases: November 3rd, 2017 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
November 9th, 2017 (Wide), released as Mord im Orient Express (Germany)
November 10th, 2017 (Wide), released as Murder On The Orient Express (Australia)
November 10th, 2017 (Wide) (Argentina)
November 10th, 2017 (Wide), released as Žmogžudystė rytų eksprese (Lithuania)
Video Release: February 20th, 2018 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for violence and thematic elements.
(Rating bulletin 2495 (Cert #51021), 9/27/2017)
Running Time: 114 minutes
Franchise: Kenneth Branagh's Hercule Poirot
Cast, crew, or production detail: Directing Yourself, Ensemble, Intertitle, Prologue, Remake
Geographical setting: Israel
Plot point: Murder Mystery, Surprise Twist, Trains
Subgenre: Mystery Drama
Time period setting: 1930s
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: The Mark Gordon Company, Scott Free Films, Kinberg Genre, 20th Century Fox
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

What starts out as a lavish train ride through Europe quickly unfolds into one of the most stylish, suspenseful and thrilling mysteries ever told. It is the tale of thirteen strangers stranded on a train, where everyone’s a suspect. One man must race against time to solve the puzzle before the murderer strikes again.
